  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Artifact makes a strong contribution claim ('first cross-lingual sentiment classifier... competitive across high- and low-resource languages'); thesis coherence and gap framing need verification against what's actually demonstrated.",
    "R1.2": "Logical tension present: paper claims to introduce the training procedure but simultaneously attributes it to Smith et al. (2022); the 'first' claim sits uneasily alongside Liu et al. (2019) who explored the same core idea—these contradictions require consistency checking.",
    "R1.3": "Quantitative claims are made (81.3% vs 78.4% on Spanish; 76.1% vs 72.8% on Mandarin) but full results are deferred to 'Table 4' which is absent from the artifact; alignment between stated numbers and the scope of the evaluation needs scrutiny.",
    "R1.4": "The 'first' superlative is a high-stakes novelty claim; cited prior work (Liu et al. 2019, Smith et al. 2022) plausibly overlaps significantly with the contribution, making delta assessment load-bearing.",
    "R1.7": "Two citations are central to the argument: Smith et al. (2022) is credited with the core training procedure the paper claims to build on, and Liu et al. (2019) is used to frame the translation-free approach; accuracy and attribution of both must be verified."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,

rationale.R1.1: Artifact makes a strong contribution claim ('first cross-lingual sentiment classifier... competitive across high- and low-resource languages'); thesis coherence and gap framing need verification against what's actually demonstrated.
rationale.R1.2: Logical tension present: paper claims to introduce the training procedure but simultaneously attributes it to Smith et al. (2022); the 'first' claim sits uneasily alongside Liu et al. (2019) who explored the same core idea—these contradictions require consistency checking.
rationale.R1.3: Quantitative claims are made (81.3% vs 78.4% on Spanish; 76.1% vs 72.8% on Mandarin) but full results are deferred to 'Table 4' which is absent from the artifact; alignment between stated numbers and the scope of the evaluation needs scrutiny.
rationale.R1.4: The 'first' superlative is a high-stakes novelty claim; cited prior work (Liu et al. 2019, Smith et al. 2022) plausibly overlaps significantly with the contribution, making delta assessment load-bearing.
rationale.R1.7: Two citations are central to the argument: Smith et al. (2022) is credited with the core training procedure the paper claims to build on, and Liu et al. (2019) is used to frame the translation-free approach; accuracy and attribution of both must be verified.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this check cover scientific substance—logical consistency, evidence-claim alignment, novelty of the 'first' claim, and citation accuracy—or is the scope limited to surface writing quality (grammar, clarity, style)?
